One-Pan Coconut-Lime Chicken

By Laura RegeUpdated: Aug 25, 2025
4.5
40 Ratings
Jump to recipe
Yields:
4 serving(s)
Prep Time:
10 mins
Total Time:
30 mins
Cal/Serv:
802

Cooking is easier on the app.

Download for free!

One-Pan Coconut-Lime Chicken
Delish US
LIVE
Current Time  0:00
Total Duration  0:23
Watch: One-Pan Coconut-Lime Chicken

One of our most-loved recipes year after year, this coconut-lime chicken is the perfect way to shake up your weeknight dinner routine with minimal effort. The method is reminiscent of our other fan-favorite recipes like creamy Tuscan chicken and Marry Me chicken: Well-seasoned chicken is cooked directly in a dreamy, creamy sauce that you’ll be thinking about for days after. For this recipe, I went with a spicy-sweet coconut milk sauce full of lime juice, jalapeño, ginger, and both tomatoes and tomato paste. The result is a super tender, flavorful chicken with a golden-brown crust, coated in the signature coconut-lime sauce. The best part? It all comes together in one pan, so cleanup is a breeze.

Another perk of this one-pan chicken is that it is totally customizable. I chose to use chicken cutlets here, but if you want larger portions, you can easily substitute in breasts (cutlets are just breasts cut in half!) and just up the cooking time slightly. Similarly, if you want to add some veggies to your meal plan, bell peppers or zucchini would go great here.

If you somehow manage to have leftovers, this dish will keep, stored in an airtight container, in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

Made this? Let us know what you think in the comments down below!

Advertisement - Continue Reading Below

Ingredients

  • 6 chicken cutlets
  • 1 tsp. sweet paprika
  • 1/4 tsp. freshly ground black pepper
  • 1 1/2 tsp. kosher salt, divided
  • 2 Tbsp. coconut oil or vegetable oil, divided
  • 1 small yellow onion, finely chopped
  • 1 small jalapeño, stemmed, seeded, finely chopped
  • 3 garlic cloves, finely chopped
  • 4 tsp. finely chopped peeled ginger
  • 2 large, ripe beefsteak tomatoes, seeded, finely chopped (about 2 cups)
  • 1 Tbsp. tomato paste
  • 1 can unsweetened coconut milk
  • 1 Tbsp. light brown sugar
  • 1/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves, coarsely chopped
  • 1 Tbsp. fresh lime juice
Off

You’ll Also Love:

Cilantro-Lime Chicken

Crunchy Coconut Chicken

Creamy Coconut-Lime Chickpea Skillet

Chicken Parmesan

Directions

    1. Step 1Season chicken all over with paprika, pepper, and 1 tsp. salt. In a large, high-sided skillet over medium-high heat, heat 1 Tbsp. oil. Working in batches, cook chicken, turning halfway through, until golden brown on both sides and just about cooked through, 1 to 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate.
    2. Step 2In same skillet over medium-high heat, heat remaining 1 Tbsp. oil. Cook onion, stirring occasionally, until slightly tender and just turning golden, about 5 minutes. Add jalapeño, garlic, and ginger and cook, stirring, until fragrant and light golden, about 1 minute more. Add tomatoes and tomato paste and cook, stirring occasionally, until tomato is softened and paste is lightly toasted, about 2 minutes more.
    3. Step 3Add milk, brown sugar, and 1/2 tsp. salt and bring to a boil, stirring until sugar is dissolved. Nestle chicken into skillet and return to a boil. Boil until chicken is cooked through, about 1 minute more; season with salt, if needed.
    4. Step 4Remove from heat. Stir in cilantro and lime juice.
I made it
